item.title: Takeaways from Rubio testimony before Senate Foreign Relations Committee
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio testified before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee Tuesday, giving an update on the war in Iran. CBS News congressional correspondent Taurean Small has more.

item.title: 3 signs that it's time to open a CD account now
item.description: Is it really worth opening a CD account now? Here are three signs indicating that it may be.

item.title: 5 signs your debt could lead to wage garnishment
item.description: Behind on your debt payments? If so, you may want to keep an eye out for signs that garnishment is on the horizon.

item.title: Video shows massive drug-smuggling tunnel connecting U.S. and Mexico
item.description: Four people were arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine through the 2,000-foot-long tunnel complete with reinforced walls, ventilation and a rail system.

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner rescheduled for July 24
item.description: Prosecutors have accused Cole Allen of attempting to assassinate the president when he allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25.

item.title: Trump signs AI executive order to give government early look at new models
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order that establishes a program for AI companies to voluntarily share powerful new models with the government before releasing them to the public. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Senior Iranian officer says return to war with U.S. seems "inevitable"
item.description: Prospects for a U.S.-Iran deal seem to dim as the war between Israel and Hezbollah grinds on despite Trump saying they agreed to stop fighting.

item.title: Drug-smuggling tunnel between U.S. and Mexico discovered under fake San Diego store, DOJ says
item.description: Authorities said they found a "sophisticated" cross-border tunnel under a fake store in San Diego used to smuggle drugs from Mexico after a monthslong Homeland Security investigation. Four suspects have been charged in the operation and authorities seized more than a ton of cocaine with an estimated value of $45 million.

item.title: Trump signs AI executive order to give government early look at new models
item.description: The order asks AI companies to share previews of powerful new models with the government before they are released to the public.

item.title: Trump names controversial housing official Bill Pulte as acting intel chief
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced he's tapping housing official Bill Pulte to serve as the acting director of national intelligence to replace Tulsi Gabbard.

item.title: Fugitive who stole identity of college grad 40 years ago pleads guilty to fraud
item.description: A fugitive who lived for more than 40 years under the stolen identity of a University of Arkansas graduate has pleaded guilty to fraud, among other charges.

item.title: Some charges dropped against woman after violent arrest video, court records show
item.description: Some of the charges against the North Carolina woman whose violent arrest video went viral were dropped, court records show. In the video, former Shelby police officer Karson Hyder can be seen repeatedly punching Cherrie Moore. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Newark mayor calls for closure of Delaney Hall immigration facility at heart of anti-ICE protests
item.description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka has called for the closure of the Delaney Hall immigration facility, which has been the subject of ongoing violent protests in New Jersey. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

item.title: Former Mossad head of counterterrorism weighs in on clashes between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: Oded Ailam, a former head of counterterrorism for Israel's Mossad and researcher at the Jerusalem Center for Security and Foreign Affairs, joins to discuss the clashes between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: Watch: Senator presses Rubio on Trump embracing China, changing views on Taiwan
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. wants to preserve the status quo in Taiwan, despite a changing shift in rhetoric on tensions with China.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says there's indications that Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is alive
item.description: State Secretary Marco Rubio testified before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on Tuesday and said that there are indications that Iran's Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is alive, expanding on the communication methods being used to negotiate with Tehran.

item.title: What are Freedom 250 and America250? Behind the 2 groups planning celebrations
item.description: A musical concert series has become a point of political contention, with performers dropping out of the series.

item.title: Northlake police warn of gas pump screw scam
item.description: The Northlake Police Department issued a warning about a new scam at gas stations that is low tech, but effective.

item.title: Trump taps Bill Pulte to replace Tulsi Gabbard as acting director of national intelligence
item.description: President Trump announced Tuesday that he's chosen Bill Pulte, the director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, to replace Tulsi Gabbard and become acting director of national intelligence. CBS News' Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: AARP CEO breaks down report showing Americans 50 and older contribute $12.5 trillion to economy
item.description: Americans 50 years and older contribute $12.5 trillion to the U.S. economy, according to a new AARP report. Dr. Myechia Minter-Jordan, the CEO of AARP, joins to break down the report.

item.title: Understanding Hezbollah's role in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran
item.description: Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group continued trading strikes in Lebanon despite President Trump's claims that both sides agreed to stop fighting. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: Israel continues war on Hezbollah in Lebanon, impacting progress in U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group continue to clash in Lebanon, a growing conflict that could impact progress in U.S.-Iran peace talks. Sebastian Usher with BBC News, a CBS News partner, has more.

item.title: Why the Trump administration is backing off from the DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Justice Department's plans for an "anti-weaponization" fund continue to face lawmaker backlash and legal battles. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Pence talks Trump's popularity in GOP and personal values in politics
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss President Trump's policies and popularity among Republicans, personal values in politics today and who he supports in the Texas Senate race.

item.title: Rubio says Trump started Iran war because of buildup of missiles, drones to defend nuclear hopes
item.description: State Secretary Marco Rubio is testifying before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, where he discussed the Iran war and the apparent military buildup that led to Operation Epic Fury in the Middle East. This comes as the Trump administration faces more backlash for the prolonged conflict.

item.title: What assets do creditors target after winning a debt lawsuit?
item.description: A court judgment can open the door to more than collection calls, so it's important to know what may be at risk.

item.title: Voters in Iowa, California head to polls for key primaries ahead of 2026 midterm elections
item.description: Voters in California and Iowa are headed to the polls on Tuesday for some key primary races, including the buzzworthy Los Angeles mayoral race. CBS News' Julie Watts and Lana Zak have more.

item.title: Legendary wine unveiled after decades hidden under castle floor
item.description: The collection once belonged to the noble Beaufort-Spontin family, who were suspected of having collaborated with the Nazis.

item.title: American soldier and British soldier die in training accident in Iraq
item.description: The U.S. and British militaries say one American soldier and one British soldier died during a training exercise in Iraq.

item.title: Austin shooting spree: 3 held after at least 12 incidents, police say
item.description: Mayor Kirk Watson said no motive has been identified and the shootings appear to be random.
